status.integration_note: This project-bound sprite package supplies the missing Core B3 Sepulcher Knight presentation candidate without changing authoritative collision, attack origins, telegraphs, state machines, content hashes, route gates, or combat code.

scope.selection_rationale: The shield-forward silhouette communicates the canonical pursue, shield-fan, and charge-lane kit while remaining visually subordinate to Sir Caldus and visibly more dangerous than the six 48-pixel Core normal enemies.

design_authority: Gravebound_Production_GDD_v1_Canonical.md:ENC-002, ENC-014, ART-001, ART-002, ART-004, ART-020, ART-030
Gravebound_Development_Roadmap_v1.md:GB-M03 private character-life route and M03 cumulative asset target

input_images.role: complete first Core enemy trio palette, pixel-cluster, camera, and readability reference only; not an edit target
complete second Core enemy trio palette, pixel-cluster, camera, and readability reference only; not an edit target
Sir Caldus boss palette and armor/shield hierarchy reference only; not an edit target

generation.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound elite/miniboss idle runtime sprite source for a native top-down 2D dark-fantasy action RPG
Primary request: Create exactly one isolated canonical Sepulcher Knight in a calm shield-forward idle guard stance. It is a Tier-I Elite/miniboss: a broad, imposing but clearly smaller and less ornate precursor to Sir Caldus, designed to pursue, brace behind its shield, fire a shield fan, and charge down a lane.
Input images: Image 1 and Image 2 are the complete existing Core enemy palette, pixel-cluster, camera, and readability references; Image 3 is the Sir Caldus boss palette and armor/shield reference. Use all only as style/scale-family references, not edit targets.
Scene/backdrop: perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for later removal; absolutely uniform edge-to-edge.
Subject: one helmeted sepulcher knight viewed from a consistent top-down three-quarter south-facing angle. Broad wet-iron plate silhouette, large battered kite/heater shield visibly forward on one side, short heavy ash-bone mace or blunt sepulchral weapon held low on the other side, closed helm with a narrow dark visor, a small cracked tarnished-brass bell reliquary on the breast or belt, restrained bone/ash devotional accents. Feet must be visible and share one stable ground line. No exposed face.
Style/medium: polished production-quality 2D pixel art; disciplined hard-edged pixel clusters, deliberately limited palette and high-contrast shape design; match the supplied Gravebound enemy sprites rather than painterly concept art. No smooth vector edges, no 3D render, no anti-aliased illustration look.
Composition/framing: exactly one full-body subject centered horizontally with generous transparent-boundary padding on every side; upright compact Elite proportions; stable bottom-center foot anchor; no cropping, no touching the canvas edge; shield and weapon remain inside the silhouette's compact bounds.
Lighting/mood: damp crypt gloom with sparse warm brass highlights; menacing, solemn, readable rather than noisy.
Color palette: charcoal, wet iron, ash black, muted bone, tarnished brass, tiny cold slate accents. Do not use magenta/pink/purple anywhere on the subject.
Materials/textures: pitted wet iron, aged brass, ash-stained bone, worn dark cloth; large material clusters that survive nearest-neighbor reduction.
Constraints: exactly one character; idle guard only; no motion streaks or attack effects; silhouette must remain distinct at a 96x96 runtime footprint; no baked shadow or floor; background must be one perfectly uniform #ff00ff with no gradients, texture, reflections, lighting variation, floor plane, or cast/contact shadow.
Avoid: extra figures, duplicate limbs, oversized boss ornament, glowing eyes, flame, fog, smoke, particles, scenery, architecture, halo, projectile, telegraph, lane, ring, UI, border, dividers, labels, text, logo, watermark, loot beam, exit ring, safe-zone ring.

postprocess.normalization: Trim the alpha>=8 bounds, nearest-neighbor fit into an 88-pixel content box, center horizontally, and bottom-align on a transparent 96x96 canvas.
postprocess.collision_authority: None. Authoritative 0.55 collision radius, 0.48 hurtbox radius, locomotion, charge, shield-fan, stop-ring, telegraph, and damage rules remain in Core content and simulation records.

validation.automated: The runtime candidate is RGBA 96x96, has transparent corners, nonempty alpha coverage, bottom-center anchoring, 3602 fully opaque pixels, 60 partially transparent pixels, and zero visible magenta-family pixels at alpha>=64.
validation.visual: Inspected at 96px, nearest-neighbor 192px, grayscale, and in an actual-source-footprint comparison with all six Core normal enemies and Sir Caldus. The broad shield-and-mace silhouette reads above normal-enemy threat while remaining materially smaller and less ornate than the boss. Wet iron, tarnished brass, ash-bone accents, feet, visor, shield edge, and weapon remain legible; no shadow, text, watermark, telegraph, projectile, loot, exit, or safe-zone cue is baked in.
validation.remaining_gate: Before registry/content-hash promotion, verify the candidate in the optimized native B3 room at 1280x720 and 1920x1080 in standard and reduced-effects modes against the Grave Arbalist, authoritative charge/fan/ring telegraphs, hostile projectiles, and the exact renderer scale.

license_provenance: Project-generated original material using OpenAI built-in image generation. Existing project imagery was supplied only as in-project visual style and hierarchy reference. Commercial-rights, similarity, repository-registry, and licensing review remain required before shipping.
